strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Lemon Man breaks the cluster on bidirectional_review, ipc_tax_deduction, wsq_score_reducer, and 21_day_reply_window — uncopyable by foreign incumbents and absent from every SG-local rail — at the deliberate cost of countries_covered, which we hold to SG-only on purpose to defend MyInfo + PDPA + WSQ + IPC structural moats.

dimensions.label: Behavioural trait data (no-show / Q4 leave / suspicious MC)

rubrics.bidirectional_review: 0 — Single-direction only (employer rates worker). 1 — Worker can flag inaccuracy via support ticket. 2 — Structured worker reply but employer-side only sees it on request. 3 — Worker reply published on the record but no decay or appeal. 4 — Worker counter-review with decay, partial appeal flow. 5 — Full bidirectional rail: workers counter-review employers AND scores propagate publicly with structured appeal.
rubrics.twenty_one_day_reply: 0 — No reply window. 1 — Ad-hoc dispute support. 2 — Reply allowed but no deadline. 3 — Reply window > 30 days, no enforcement. 4 — 21-day reply window codified, partial auto-escalation. 5 — 21-day reply window mirrored on MAS norm, enforced at policy level with auto-escalation.
rubrics.free_worker_appeal: 0 — Worker cannot appeal. 1 — Appeal exists but paywalled (e.g. ReputationDefender). 2 — One free appeal then paid. 3 — Free appeal for factual errors only. 4 — Free appeal across all claim types, manual review. 5 — Free appeal with structured rehabilitation pathway (claim → review → decay or correction).
rubrics.wsq_score_reducer: 0 — No link to training. 1 — Training mentioned generically. 2 — Worker can attach training cert manually. 3 — WSQ Statement of Attainment recognised but no score impact. 4 — WSQ enrolment / completion triggers score decay. 5 — WSQ enrolment + completion + SkillsFuture funding routed into a documented score-decay curve, partner-integrated.
rubrics.ipc_tax_deduction: 0 — No charity link. 1 — Charity links visible. 2 — Donation tracking but no tax angle. 3 — IPC donation recognised but no score impact. 4 — IPC donation triggers score decay. 5 — IPC donation at 250% IRAS deduction + Lemon Score reduction tightly bound; 3-5 named IPC partners with ladder ladders.
rubrics.transparent_sgd_pricing: 0 — Hidden enterprise quote only. 1 — Pricing available on request. 2 — Pricing partially published (per-check from $X). 3 — Tier pricing published in USD. 4 — Tier pricing published in SGD, some bundles undisclosed. 5 — Full SGD tier list published; per-lookup ≤ S$10; all bundles transparent.
rubrics.singpass_myinfo: 0 — No identity verification. 1 — Email/password only. 2 — National ID upload manual. 3 — Third-party IDV (Yoti, Onfido). 4 — Singpass login supported, MyInfo data pull partial. 5 — Singpass MyInfo Day-1 architectural primitive; verified workers get score-floor modifier.
rubrics.tafep_wfa_alignment: 0 — No SG fair-employment alignment. 1 — Generic equal-opportunity statement. 2 — TAFEP mentioned in legal page. 3 — TAFEP-aligned audit log on request. 4 — TAFEP + TGFEP audit module bundled in mid-tier. 5 — TAFEP + WFA dispute-export bundle native; pre-built for end-2027 WFA effective date.
rubrics.shift_sector_fit: 0 — White-collar pre-hire only. 1 — Generic background checks not fitted to shift. 2 — Shift-sector clients exist incidentally. 3 — Shift sector named in marketing. 4 — Shift sector is primary ICP; F&B/security/MICE language native. 5 — Built for shift sector first; F&B PWM cost math, AOG penalties, MICE burst-hiring all explicitly priced.
rubrics.behavioural_trait_data: 0 — Identity / credential only. 1 — Generic employment dates verified. 2 — Performance review snippets. 3 — Limited trait flagging (criminal only). 4 — Structured trait taxonomy (no-show, late, last-minute bail). 5 — Five-trait Lemon taxonomy (no-show, last-minute bail, suspicious MC, Q4 leave-clearer, data-secrecy) with corroboration.
rubrics.turnaround_speed: 0 — > 30 days. 1 — 15-30 days. 2 — 10-15 days. 3 — 5-10 days. 4 — Same-day to 48 hours. 5 — Real-time lookup (< 5 seconds) on a queryable database.
rubrics.sg_data_residency: 0 — US-only data hosting. 1 — Global hosting, no SG region. 2 — APAC region but not SG. 3 — SG region available on enterprise. 4 — SG region default. 5 — SG-only data residency, PDPA Schedule 1 + Singpass MyInfo trust chain documented.

segments.name: SG F&B Multi-Outlet Chain
segments.descriptor: 8–20 outlets · 120–200 shift staff · F&B PWM-anchored (S$12.09/hr 2025); ops director fighting daily no-shows

segments.name: SG PLRD-Licensed Security Agency
segments.descriptor: 50–500 officers · S$200-500 SLA penalty per missed deployment; MAS-style regime extension on PLRD horizon

segments.name: SG MICE / Events Staffing Planner
segments.descriptor: SACEOS/AEO-SG member · burst 50–200 freelance crew per weekend; single no-show = unbackfillable

segments.name: SG Dormitory / Construction Agency
segments.descriptor: 200–1,000 work-permit holders · S$1,072 AWOL/runaway loss; BCA + MOM conduct-trail tightening

segments.name: SG Retail / E-com Fulfilment Ops
segments.descriptor: 5–15 outlets / 3PL warehouse · peak-season variable demand; retail PWM S$12.09/hr 2025

segments.name: SG Top-50 Staffing Agency
segments.descriptor: Surviving 300 active EAs (down from 3,900 in 2016); CEI/AEAS-trained recruiters; wholesale-channel buyer

segments.name: SG Logistics / Warehouse Ops
segments.descriptor: 3,200 reachable logistics ops · WSG Logistics ITM; shift roster across last-mile + warehouse

segments.name: SG Worker (Self-Claim)
segments.descriptor: 744K shift/contract workers · Singpass-fluent · expects redemption pathway; price-sensitive (free side)

segments.name: SG FI Non-FI Compliance Overflow
segments.descriptor: MAS-FI staff who also hire non-FI vendors / contractors; carry the FI mental model into adjacent purchases

segments.name: SG WFA-Bound 25+ Headcount Hirer
segments.descriptor: Firms with 25+ staff facing end-2027 Workplace Fairness Act dispute-export duty; cross-sector

needs.name: 5-trait Lemon taxonomy (no-show / bail / MC / Q4 / data)

attack_plans.niche_name: Bidirectional rail no SG competitor offers
attack_plans.icp: SG F&B Multi-Outlet Chain ops director (8–20 outlets, 120–200 shift staff) currently running an illegal employer WhatsApp group; needs a bidirectional, PDPA-clean reference rail with worker counter-review.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Agent 2 SAM stack: ~9,400 reachable F&B SMEs × S$2,400 avg annual spend = S$22.6M. Lemon Man targets ~5% Year-3 share (~470 chains × S$2,400) tied to F&B-side primary-value persona (Agent 3 fnb_chain_ops_director).
attack_plans.why_gap: No SG-local or SEA competitor offers bidirectional review. RMI, Sterling, HireRight, eeCheck, Veremark are all single-direction. WhatsApp / agency Sheets are PDPA-fragile and one-way. FastGig keeps ratings closed-loop. Bidirectional is uncopyable in SG without a structurally new product.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on bidirectional_review + 5 on twenty_one_day_reply + 5 on free_worker_appeal + 5 on shift_sector_fit. Worker counter-review codifies a TAFEP-aligned dispute mechanism that simultaneously gives the F&B ops director a PDPA-safe alternative to the illegal WhatsApp group. Lemon Man's bidirectional rail is also pre-built WFA-compliance.

gtm.channel: Founder-led WhatsApp onboarding (5–10 F&B chains/wk Y1) + Singapore Food Manufacturers Association (SFMA) + Restaurant Association of Singapore (RAS) member webinars + the SG F&B operator Telegram groups Lemon Man is replacing.
gtm.pitch: Stop running an illegal blacklist WhatsApp group. Get a PDPA-clean reference rail where workers can counter-review you — and watch your no-show rate drop without exposing your chain to TAFEP.
gtm.pricing: Operator tier S$299/mo list, S$149.50/mo net post-PSG; per-lookup S$7 PAYG sits below the CBS S$8 anchor.
content: RAS / SFMA case study with 3 early F&B chains: hours saved + no-shows deflected; co-branded with NTUC FAST
Ungated 'How Singapore F&B WhatsApp blacklists violate PDPA Section 17 (and what to do instead)' explainer
On-demand 20-minute webinar with a TAFEP-aware HR lawyer on bidirectional referencing
F&B-specific deflected-incident ROI calculator on the pricing page (one S$420 no-show = 60× ROI on S$7 lookup)

attack_plans.icp: SG worker (744K shift/contract/freelance pool) seeking a redemption pathway; Forward SG mental model expects 'I made a mistake — what's the way back?' — also the SG hirer who wants to point employees at a culturally-aligned decay pathway.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Agent 2 SAM 'cross-buyer enterprise + bundled-IPC residue' stack = S$36M / yr. Hirer-side IPC redemption flow as a bundle is the volume lever. Workers stay free; IPC-route engagement raises retention, reduces churn, drives hirer Operator/Agency tier ARPU upgrades. Year-3 ~S$1.8M attributable to IPC-bundled retention.
attack_plans.why_gap: Checkr, Sterling, HireRight, GoodHire, Veremark cannot replicate this without a SG entity + IRAS-recognised IPC integrations. RMI / eeCheck / SEEK Pass do not tie tax-deductible giving to demerit decay. The IRAS 250% deduction sunsets 31 Dec 2026 — uncopyable structural moat with a launch forcing function.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on ipc_tax_deduction + 5 on wsq_score_reducer + 5 on bidirectional_review + 4 on singpass_myinfo. Lemon Man partners with 3–5 named IPCs (NCSS-aligned causes) and routes a tiered donation ladder (S$100 / S$500 / S$2,000) into the documented score-decay curve. The same flow doubles as Forward SG narrative content for the Q3 2026 SSG/WSG merger press cycle.

gtm.channel: Worker-side: Giving.sg + SG Cares + 3–5 named NCSS-aligned IPC partner micro-sites + Singpass MyInfo deep-link. Hirer-side: bundled with Operator/Agency tier as 'IPC Engagement Discount' (10% platform fee rebate capped S$300/mo).
gtm.pitch: Donate S$200 to a certified IPC. Get S$500 back at IRAS. Reduce your Lemon Score the same day. (Until 31 Dec 2026.)
gtm.pricing: Worker side free forever. Hirer side: Operator tier S$299/mo gets a 10% rebate when employees visibly route into IPC flows.
content: Worker-page hero: S$200 donate → S$500 tax relief → score decay calculator with timer counting down to 31 Dec 2026
Press release: 'Lemon Man partners with [3–5 IPCs] to underwrite SG's first behavioural-credit redemption rail'
Forward SG narrative explainer mapping Lemon Man's redemption flow to the social-compact refresh
PDPC + IRAS legal one-pager confirming the IPC + Lemon Score loop is compliant with both regimes

attack_plans.niche_name: WSQ-to-score bridge with Q3 2026 SSG-WSG merger
attack_plans.icp: SG worker enrolled (or enrolling) in a SkillsFuture-funded WSQ programme; secondary buyer is the SG F&B chain / security agency / dormitory operator who wants to retain workers by surfacing the score-decay pathway through training.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Year-3 attributable: ~30% of Lemon Man's hirer-side base (~285 chains + 36 agencies) buys the WSQ-bundled Operator at +S$50/mo upgrade × 12 + WSQ training-provider referral revenue share at ~5% × ~S$3M directed-training spend ≈ S$0.6–0.7M Year-3 ARR. Cumulative 3-year ~S$1.4M direct; underwrites ~S$14M of WSQ-funded training routed through Lemon Man's worker portal.
attack_plans.why_gap: SkillsFuture + WSG sit at universal adult reach but only as training rails — they do not productise behavioural decay. SEEK Pass surfaces WSQ on the verified-credential page but never ties it to a reliability score the hirer sees. No competitor connects (a) WSQ enrolment → (b) Lemon Score decay → (c) hirer-side retention signal.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on wsq_score_reducer + 5 on bidirectional_review + 4 on singpass_myinfo + 5 on shift_sector_fit. Q3 2026 SSG/WSG merger announcement (Aug-Sep 2026) is the launch forcing function — Lemon Man positions as the demand-signal rail the merged statutory board reaches for. Zero-CAC margin: government pays for the WSQ training, Lemon Man captures the redemption flow.

gtm.channel: Direct integration with MySkillsFuture portal + WSQ training-provider partner network + NTUC FAST member-rate co-marketing + timed launch comms with Q3 2026 SSG/WSG merger announcement.
gtm.pitch: Enrol in a WSQ programme — SkillsFuture pays, your Lemon Score drops. The hirer hiring you next sees you on a comeback path, not a blacklist.
gtm.pricing: Worker-side free. Hirer-side: Operator tier WSQ Enrolment Discount 10% rebate (capped S$300/mo) when employees visibly route into WSQ programmes; Agency Wholesale 15% lookup rebate on placed-worker WSQ flow.
content: Co-branded landing page with 3–5 WSQ training providers showing 'enrol → decay → re-hire' flow
Q3 2026 SSG/WSG merger press hook: 'The first private platform that routes lemons into WSQ retraining'
Worker-facing redemption explainer in English / Mandarin / Bahasa / Tagalog
Hirer-facing retention case study: chains using WSQ-bundled Lemon Man see X% lower 90-day quit rate

attack_plans.niche_name: 21-day reply window — MAS norm for non-FI
attack_plans.icp: SG PLRD-licensed security agency operations manager (50–500 officers) facing AOG-style SLA penalties and the credible MAS-extension trajectory onto PLRD; needs MAS-grade procedural fairness for non-FI hires.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Agent 2 SAM stack: ~700 security buyers × S$6,000 avg annual spend = S$4.2M. Lemon Man captures 30% Year-3 (~210 agencies × S$6,000) tied to security_agency_manager persona, which Agent 3 tags as low-elasticity (MAS-style PLRD extension expected, AOG penalty floor, CEI/AEAS-trained recruiter context).
attack_plans.why_gap: MAS regime is FI-only. No non-FI vendor (RMI, Sterling, HireRight, Veremark, eeCheck, FastJobs, Workmate) codifies a 21-day reply window with auto-escalation for shift-sector hires. The 21-day norm is the cultural seed; no platform transplants it into the security / dormitory / construction perimeter.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on twenty_one_day_reply + 5 on free_worker_appeal + 4 on tafep_wfa_alignment + 5 on behavioural_trait_data. WFA-Ready Enterprise tier mirrors the MAS schema (work history, balanced-scorecard analogue, persistency-ratio analogue) for shift-sector compliance — and the 21-day enforcement is the PR-defensible response to any 'this is a blacklist' accusation.

gtm.channel: Security Association of Singapore (SAS) member webinar + NTUC LearningHub CEI / AEAS partner page + direct outreach to surviving 250 PLRD-licensed agencies + targeted LinkedIn ads to 'Operations Manager' at agencies with 100+ officers.
gtm.pitch: MAS made the 21-day reply window the standard for banks. Lemon Man is the first to make it the standard for the security sector — so when PLRD adopts the same regime, your agency is already on the rail.
gtm.pricing: WFA-Ready Enterprise S$1,250/mo list (S$15,000/yr); S$625/mo net post-PSG; EDG-bundleable when delivered with implementation + training.
content: Comparative one-pager: 'MAS reference-check regime vs Lemon Man procedural fairness'
SAS webinar with a former MAS supervisor on what PLRD extension looks like in 2027
ROI calculator: 200 officers × 3% lemon × S$650 SLA penalty × 40 events/yr = S$156k/yr deflectable
WFA Readiness Audit lead-gen content (Q4 2026 launch ahead of end-2027 WFA effective date)

attack_plans.icp: Principal / GM of an SG dormitory or construction staffing agency (200–1,000 work-permit holders) inside the surviving 300 active EAs; carries main-contractor procurement pressure for documented vetting trails.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Agent 2 SAM stack: 300 active staffing agencies × S$9,000 ARPU = S$2.7M direct agency channel + 1,600 dormitory + construction operators × S$4,500 ARPU = S$7.2M residue. Combined channel-leverage rail ~S$10M. Lemon Man captures ~30% Year-3 of the active agency subset (~90 agencies @ S$5,000-9,000 ARPU) per Agent 3 channel pricing.
attack_plans.why_gap: RMI / Sterling / HireRight / Accurate sell project-shape, not agency wholesale. SpringVerify / IDfy are India-anchored, no MOM accreditation, no agency rebate stream. Agency blocklist Sheets are siloed per agency with no cross-agency corroboration. No competitor offers a white-labelled, rebate-bearing wholesale API for the surviving top-50 agencies.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on shift_sector_fit + 5 on behavioural_trait_data + 5 on bidirectional_review + 5 on transparent_sgd_pricing. Channel-leverage compresses sales cycle from 6 months to 6 weeks per Agent 2's market intel; agency rebate stream (15% rebate on lookups placed workers trigger at downstream hirers) creates a recurring revenue ladder agencies have never seen.

gtm.channel: MOM EA Directory top-50 outbound; AEAS member webinars; NTUC LearningHub CEI/AEAS partner page; Corestaff 2026 industry review reprint with Lemon Man embedded as 'the wholesale channel agencies have been asking for'.
gtm.pitch: Your branch Sheets are siloed. Your CEI-certified recruiters burn 15 hr/wk chasing references. Plug into Lemon Man's wholesale API, white-label it inside your ATS, and earn a 15% rebate on every downstream lookup your placed workers trigger.
gtm.pricing: Agency Wholesale tier S$2,400/mo list (S$28,800/yr); S$1,200/mo net post-PSG (subject to IMDA pre-approval); 15% rebate stream returns ~25–40% of platform fee within 12 months.
content: Corestaff 2026 industry-review co-branded teardown with Lemon Man wholesale tier embedded
Top-50 EA outbound deck: 'Why your branch Sheets are a PDPA disclosure trap'
Rebate calculator: 400-worker roster × 4 downstream lookups/yr × S$7 × 15% = S$1,680/yr rebate stream
White-label co-brand asset pack (logos, embed code, ATS integration guide)

attack_plans.icp: SG MICE / events staffing planner (SACEOS / AEO-SG member) burst-hiring 50–200 freelance crew per weekend; carries unbackfillable event-day failure risk and irregular subscription appetite.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Agent 2 SAM stack: ~1,400 MICE / event ops × S$3,500 avg annual spend = S$4.9M. Per-lookup model captures spike-week volume that subscription models miss; Year-3 ~S$1.5M attributable at ~30% of MICE SAM.
attack_plans.why_gap: Subscription-shaped competitors (RMI, Sterling, Veremark, FastJobs, Workmate) misfit the burst-week cadence — flat monthly fees feel wasteful in quiet weeks and absurd in peak weeks. WhatsApp groups are the default but carry no corroboration count for event-day decisions. No competitor offers per-lookup-only with SACEOS-grade trust.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on transparent_sgd_pricing + 5 on sub_48hr_turnaround + 5 on shift_sector_fit + 5 on behavioural_trait_data + 5 on bidirectional_review. Pay-as-you-go tier at S$7/lookup mirrors Agent 3 pricing_models.score_by_persona where per-lookup scores 5/5 for MICE staffing planners; Lemon Man dominates an under-served burst-week niche.

gtm.channel: SACEOS member roster outbound; AEO-SG webinar slot; F1 / Art Week / NDP / Black Friday event-week paid search; targeted LinkedIn ads to 'Production Manager' / 'Staffing Lead' at SG event agencies.
gtm.pitch: F1 weekend. Art Week. NDP. Black Friday. Single no-show on event day = unbackfillable. S$7 per lookup. Sub-5-second answer. No subscription. No commitment. Just deflect the lemons.
gtm.pricing: Pay-as-you-go S$7/lookup + S$20/upload; no minimum; top-up credits expire 12 months. PAYG-side margin is volume-driven not commitment-driven.
content: SACEOS event-week playbook: 'How to vet 200 burst-hire crew in 48 hours'

Per-lookup pricing-page hero math: 1 deflected MICE no-show (S$800 loss) = 114× ROI

attack_plans.icp: SG retail / e-commerce fulfilment ops lead (5–15 outlets / 3PL warehouse) running PWM-anchored peak-season cycles; price-sensitive, high-elasticity, rejects anything above CBS S$8 anchor.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Agent 2 SAM stack: ~5,500 reachable retail multi-outlet ops × S$1,800 avg annual spend = S$9.9M. High-elasticity segment per Agent 3; capture at the floor or not at all. Year-3 ~S$2M attributable at ~20% of retail SAM via per-lookup + low-commit Operator tier.
attack_plans.why_gap: >40% of incumbents are 'hidden_estimated' on the pricing flag (Agent 1 evidence): Sterling, HireRight, Accurate, Cisive, Onfido, Persona, Sumsub, IDfy, SpringVerify, Workmate, Sampingan, ShiftMatch, Talentvis, RecruitFirst, First Advantage, Trulioo. Procurement-driven retail ops leads reject quote-based products on principle. Transparent SGD per-lookup ≤ S$10 is structural whitespace.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on transparent_sgd_pricing + 5 on shift_sector_fit + 5 on behavioural_trait_data + 5 on sub_48hr_turnaround. Lemon Man publishes all four tiers (S$7 PAYG, S$299 Operator, S$2,400 Agency, S$15k/yr WFA-Enterprise) on a single SGD pricing card with the PSG-funded effective price next to list — a posture no global incumbent will copy without a SG entity + IMDA pre-approval.

gtm.channel: Retail multi-outlet outbound (Cold Storage / FairPrice / Sheng Siong / NTUC FAST member rate); Singapore Retailers Association (SRA) member discount; SBF NBS 2025 'retail at bottom on optimism' cohort retargeting.
gtm.pitch: Tired of 'contact sales for pricing'? Lemon Man publishes every tier in SGD on one page — and shows you the PSG-funded effective price next to list. Less than CBS's S$8 consumer credit report. Built for retail PWM math.
gtm.pricing: PAYG S$7/lookup; Operator S$299/mo (S$149.50 net post-PSG). The pricing card carries CBS price comparison + retail PWM ROI math.
content: SRA-co-branded retail-PWM ROI calculator: 8 outlets × 18 shift staff × 2% lemon × S$340 loss = S$3,400/mo deflectable
